
**STF Project**

Starting in summer 2025, the [R]{.sans-serif} Foundation is working on 
a series of work packages improving the long-term stability of the [R]{.sans-serif}
ecosystem. The project is supported by the Sovereign Tech Agency
<https://www.sovereign.tech/>. In a series of blog posts, some details about
the achieved results will be shared with the public, starting with Milestone
1. These blog posts are a tribute to the late TomÃ¡Å¡ Kalibera, who
continued working on this project during his last months. 

**Milestone 1: Triage and reduce backlog of bugs**

At the beginning of the project, base [R]{.sans-serif} over $600$ open bugs in its issue
tracking system bugzilla. This work involved systematically analysing the backlog of bugs,
identifying those that are not relevant and can be closed, and
researching solutions for significant bugs then implementing fixes that
resolve the issues. The quality of the code base was thus improved,
increasing the visibility of reported problems which are significant in
the future and improving bug management for its maintainer.

**Milestone Results**

On July 31, 2025, there were $624$ open bug reports in the R-project
bugzilla bug tracker (<https://bugs.r-project.org>). It has been reduced
to $467$ open bug reports on October 1, 2025, which is a reduction of
about $25\%$. Bugs no longer relevant or not reproducible/actionable
were closed with a short explanation. Invalid bugs and issues not
feasible to fix/not worth fixing, as well wishlist items not worth
adding were closed with a detailed explanation, so that the people
reporting the bugs aren't discouraged from further cooperation, but
instead learn how to better contribute. Some still relevant bugs were
fixed, including bugs many years old. In addition to that, comments to
some (still) open bugs were added in an attempt to get further
information from the reporters or expert comments, increasing the
chances they will be resolved, soon. This effort has been noticed by
package developers who also reported some of the bugs and positively
reflected on the R-devel mailing list:

<https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-devel/2025-August/084155.html>\
<https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-devel/2025-August/084157.html>

The results of the work are publicly available on
<https://bugs.r-project.org>, where the advanced search feature allows
to focus on specific activity in the two months allocated for M1, the
result is available as
<https://bugs.r-project.org/buglist.cgi?chfield=resolution&chfieldfrom=2025-08-01&chfieldto=2025-09-30&order=Last%20Changed&product=R&query_format=advanced>.
